summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering agenda adoption, bill payments, zoning text amendments, rezoning actions, and personnel matters. Three zoning resolutions were adopted: removing the 2,000 sq ft accessory structure cap in residential districts, establishing a 20% non-residential requirement for PDM mixed-use districts, and rezoning the former Combs dump site from M-2 to B-2. The Board also approved a PUCO objection letter regarding Duke Energy rate increases, authorized the prosecutor to pursue four zoning enforcement cases, approved an SRO MOU with Goshen Local Schools, and purchased a new police interceptor vehicle. Tim Hodges was named Grand Marshal for the Memorial Day Parade.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for April 14–28, 2026
Approved letter to PUCO objecting to Duke Energy distribution rate increase
Added FY2027 tax budget hearing to May 26, 2026 regular meeting agenda
Approved School Resource Officer MOU with Goshen Local Schools for 2026-2027
Approved purchase of Ford Explorer Police Interceptor from Lebanon Ford not to exceed $46,197
Named Tim Hodges as Memorial Day Parade Grand Marshal (Hodges abstained)
Adopted Resolution R-014-2026 removing 2,000 sq ft max aggregate floor area for accessory structures in residential districts
Adopted Resolution R-015-2026 establishing 20% non-residential requirement for PDM mixed-use district with 60% phasing threshold
Adopted Resolution R-016-2026 rezoning former Combs dump site parcels from M-2 to B-2 General Business District
Adopted Resolution R-017-2026 authorizing prosecutor enforcement against 1455 Woodville Pike (Axtreme Tree Service) for zoning violations
Adopted Resolution R-018-2026 authorizing prosecutor enforcement against 1777A Parker Road (Soda Four Corp/Rick Royce) for junk/inoperable vehicles
Adopted Resolution R-019-2026 authorizing prosecutor enforcement against Haywood Utility Contractors at 1800 State Route 28
Adopted Resolution R-020-2026 authorizing prosecutor enforcement against 1851 Parker Road for storage/dumping violations
Amended Clarence Groves BZA alternate member term expiration date to April 14, 2028
Entered executive session per ORC 121.22 G1
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Jeffrey Schott effective April 16, 2026
Terminated part-time firefighter paramedic Chris Sullivan effective April 28, 2026
action_items: Administrator Rose to send PUCO objection letter regarding Duke Energy rate increase
Laura and staff to prepare FY2027 tax budget for May 26 hearing
Donna Rosick to coordinate Memorial Day Parade with Tim Hodges as Grand Marshal and Administrator Rose as guest speaker
Director Langenheim to refer four zoning violation cases to Clermont County Prosecuting Attorney
Director Langenheim to pursue administrative search warrant for 1777A Parker Road with prosecutor's office
Chief Rhodes to coordinate police interceptor purchase from Lebanon Ford
Chief Myers to finalize Station 18 move-in (Thursday or Tuesday pending card access and IT)
Correct Clarence Groves BZA alternate term expiration to April 14, 2028
key_amounts: $46,197 - not to exceed cost for new Ford Explorer Police Interceptor from Lebanon Ford
$5,000 - savings from HR Director Melinda Eichenhorst completing Ohio Bureau of Workers Comp online training (25+ hours)
$100 - Lebanon Ford came under state bid price for police vehicle
notable_topics: PUCO objection letter opposing Duke Energy distribution rate increase
FY2027 tax budget hearing requirement due to Ohio law changes
School Resource Officer MOU renewal for 2026-2027
Memorial Day Parade planning - Tim Hodges named Grand Marshal
Zoning text amendment removing 2,000 sq ft accessory structure size cap (R-014-2026)
PDM mixed-use district 20% non-residential requirement and 60% phasing threshold (R-015-2026)
Rezoning former Combs dump site from M-2 to B-2 per Clermont County Land Bank request (R-016-2026)
Four zoning enforcement cases referred to prosecutor (Axtreme Tree Service, Parker Road junk vehicles, Haywood Utility, Parker Road dumping)
Station 18 firehouse near completion and move-in imminent
New quint fire apparatus chassis ready with cab painted
Condolences to Service Director Chad Meadows on death of his father
New land use plan officially in effect and moratorium lifted
BZA alternate member term correction for Clarence Groves
Personnel actions: resignation of Jeffrey Schott and termination of Chris Sullivan
attendees: Chad Meadows (Service Director - absent due to family death)
notes: Tim Hodges is the son of former Trustee Tim Hodges who served 20 years and is a U.S. Army veteran. The meeting included light-hearted banter about the Goshen Chamber bowling fundraiser at East Gate Lanes. Station 18 firehouse public opening planned for Saturday with a push-in ceremony tradition. The former Combs dump site cleanup by Clermont County Land Bank was praised. Ohio law change regarding tax budget was caused by legislature overriding governor's veto, giving County Budget Commission authority to reduce township tax collections. Jeffrey Schott resigned and Chris Sullivan was terminated from the fire department. Administrator Rose was referred to as both 'Administrator Rose' and 'Chief Rose' during the meeting.
title: BOT 4-28-26
